reasoning: The option-adjusted spread (OAS) for the ICE BofA Emerging Markets High Yield Corporate Plus Index is currently at 3.23 (as of Jan 16, 2026, the last trading day before the forecast date of Jan 18). This is a very tight spread, near the 1-year low, representing a sharp drop from 3.42 just 9 days ago. Because bond spreads generally exhibit mean reversion over time and cannot drop below zero (with tight floors dictated by default risk), there is a strong asymmetric risk pointing towards an increase (widening). Our FRED trend analysis estimates a 71% chance of an increase to return to the trendline by late January. According to standard mathematical properties of such mean-reverting processes, a higher variance at more distant dates pushes the probability closer to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends. Thus, we project a 63% probability of the spread being higher than its current value by Jan 25, fading to a 55% probability by Feb 17 in observance of increasing long-term uncertainty.
